data.title: Union Pacific locomotive engineers ratify new contract with pay hikes, stronger benefits
data.description:
• The 6,000 members of the locomotive engineers union have agreed to ratify a five-year contract with Union Pacific Corp (NYSE:UNP) providing for higher wages, improved health and welfare benefits, and accelerated vacation accrual policies.
  • Among the terms of the new contract, Union Pacific (NYSE:UNP) engineers will receive raises of 18.8% over the life of the agreement and preserves the 11/4 work-rest agreement which provides 4 days off after 11 consecutive working days.
  • The contract also increases the allowance for meals away from home, an optional, reduced-cost medical plan, and caps on monthly cost-sharing for new and existing medical coverage.
  • Since the beginning of the year, over 12,600 Union Pacific (NYSE:UNP) employees have ratified new contracts.

data.title: ProFrac Holding files to sell 3.17M shares of Class A common stock for holders
data.description:
• ProFrac Holding (NASDAQ:ACDC) filed a prospectus for the offer and sale of 3.17M shares of Class A common stock by the selling stockholders.

  •  

Filing.

data.title: Jaguar Health registers 2.62M shares for resale following private placements
data.description:
• Jaguar Health (NASDAQ:JAGX) registered 2.62M shares of common stock for resale by existing stockholders.
  • Includes 1.98M shares issuable upon exchange of 950.8 shares of Series N Preferred Stock.
  • The Series N Preferred Stock was issued in a private placement closed on September 10, 2025.
  • Also includes 161,583 shares of common stock and 479,442 shares issuable via a pre-funded warrant.
  • These additional shares stem from a second private placement closed on September 28, 2025.
  • All shares may be resold by the participating selling stockholders, not the company itself.
  • Source: SEC filing

data.title: Grupo Aeroportuario del Pacifico reports September passenger traffic increase of 1%
data.description:
• Grupo Aeroportuario del Pacífico (NYSE:PAC) on Friday said the total number of terminal passengers at GAP’s 12 Mexican airports increased by 1% in September 2025, compared to September 2024.
  • Puerto Vallarta and Guadalajara airports presented an increase in passenger traffic of 2.6% and 0.3%, respectively, while Los Cabos, Tijuana and Montego Bay airports decreased 4.8%, 0.6%, and 0.3%, respectively, compared to September 2024.
  • The seats available during September 2025 increased by 1.3%, compared to September 2024.
  • The load factors for the month went from 81.0% in September 2024 to 80.7% in September 2025.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Humana rises over 15% in last two trading sessions
data.description:
• After closing Thursday up ~4%, Humana (NYSE:HUM) ended Friday advancing another ~11%.
  • The nice gain comes after the health insurer Thursday reaffirmed its 2025 financial outlook and also stated that its Medicare Advantage plans have an average Star rating of 3.61 for 2026, in line with its 2025 data.

data.title: Pembina near deal with Meta to build massive data center in Alberta - report
data.description:
Pembina Pipeline (NYSE:PBA) closed +6% on Friday after The Logic reported the company is close to announcing a deal to build a massive AI data center in Alberta for tech giant Meta (META).

Pembina (NYSE:PBA) will work with Kineticor, which provides power generation facilities, to supply natural gas-fired electricity to the project, and data center Beacon AI also is believed to be involved in the deal, according to the report.

The Meta (META) facility would be located in Alberta's Industrial Heartland, a cluster of oil refineries, petrochemical plants and other facilities near Fort Saskatchewan, and the area also is a hub for carbon capture and storage technology, which could help tech firms drive down AI-related carbon emissions.

Pembina (PBA) reportedly would provide gas to the facility through its Alliance pipeline network connecting Alberta and northern British Columbia and Alberta to Chicago; the company said in July it was gauging the potential to expand the pipeline to feed 350 million cf/day of gas to the Industrial Heartland, coming online in 2029.

data.title: UnitedHealth shareholder wants board chair independent from CEO
data.description:
• The Accountability Board, which owns shares in about 200 companies and promotes shareholder advocacy, is asking UnitedHealth Group (NYSE:UNH) to change its bylaws to stop its CEO from also being board chairman.
  • The group has filed a formal proposal.
  • "We were very disturbed to see recently that at such a trying time for UnitedHealth, the company chose to go backward and combine the CEO and chair roles,” Accountability Board President Matt Prescott said, Bloomberg reported. "We think the company needs a stronger leadership structure."
  • In May, Stephen Hemsley, who was board chairman at the time, also took over as CEO following the sudden resignation of Andrew Witty. 
  • UnitedHealth stock has struggled in 2025, and is down ~29% year to date.

data.title: XPeng falls after seven sessions of gain
data.description:
XPeng (NYSE:XPEV) shares fell 2.88% to close at $23.13 on Friday, breaking a seven-session winning streak that had seen the stock climb steadily over the past week.

Despite today’s decline, shares remain up 14.31% over the past month and have surged 95.35% so far this year, significantly outperforming the broader market.

However, investor sentiment turned cautious after Tesla (TSLA) posted record third-quarter deliveries, beating expectations and setting a new quarterly high. Tesla’s surge was partly driven by a rush to secure the recently expired $7,500 federal EV tax credit. The company also reported strong demand in China, with its updated Model Y and new six-seat Model Y L variant capturing a growing share of the market.

Tesla’s dominant performance and plans to expand its footprint in China have raised concerns among investors about the competitive pressures facing local players that include Xpeng.

Meanwhile, analysts sentiment remain broadly positive. Looking at Seeking Alpha’s Quant Rating, XPEV holds a Hold recommendation with an average score of 3.3 out of 5. The company scored A+ in both growth and momentum, while receiving a D- for valuation and profitability. Revisions were rated B, indicating a generally favorable shift in outlook.

Seeking Alpha analysts rate the stock a Buy with an average score of 3.75.

Wall Street sentiment remains bullish, with 15 analysts giving the stock a Strong Buy, and 6 analysts recommending a Buy. Meanwhile, 6 analysts suggest holding the stock, and 1 has issued a Sell rating.

XPeng’s record September deliveries, up 94.7% year-over-year to 41,581 units, highlight its accelerating market share gains and solidify its position as a leading growth player in China’s competitive EV landscape.

However, an analyst says the rally is "well-deserved," pointing out the company's accelerating international growth, narrowing losses, and a potential bottom-line inflection, but advises caution as a pullback to the $19 range "may potentially occur."

data.title: Wall Street ended mixed as investors navigate the ongoing government shutdown
data.description:
Wall Street’s broader market averages closed mixed on Friday as market participants digested the U.S. government shutdown for the third day.

Pushing higher was the blue-chip Dow (DJI) as it closed up by 0.51%. Also, the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) finished near even, and the tech focused Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) suffered the most as it ended lower by 0.28%. 

For the week, the Dow, S&P 500, and Nasdaq Composite advanced by 1.1%, 1.08%, and 1.32%, respectively.

“Another very positive week in equities and crypto this week, federal government shutdown notwithstanding. The wall of worry is getting steeper, as calls of ‘this is the top!’ get louder and more commonplace.  But if the supply of money continues to increase, and the cost of money continues to fall, then most likely the price of risk assets continues to climb.  We continue to look higher into year end,” Seeking Alpha analyst Alex King of Cestrian Capital Research said.

On a sector-by-sector stance, sevenof the 11 S&P segments concluded higher, as Utilities led the way. In reverse, the Consumer Discretionary segment had struggled the most and was the weakest sector on the session. 

U.S. Treasury yields ticked higher as the longer-end U.S. 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) moved up 3 basis points to 4.12% and the shorter-end U.S. 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) pushed up 3 basis points to 3.57%.

The U.S. government shutdown began at midnight Wednesday after Republicans and Democrats failed to agree on a federal funding deal. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent told CNBC on Thursday that the shutdown could weigh on GDP growth.

So far, Wall Street has largely brushed off the impasse. On prediction market Kalshi, the probability of the shutdown lasting more than 10 days rose to 69%.

With federal agencies closed, investors will be left without the September nonfarm payrolls report—a release widely viewed as critical for the Federal Reserve’s assessment of labor market conditions.

In other economic news, S&P Global US Services PMI business activity index came in at 54.2 in September, down from 54.5 in August.

The U.S. ISM Services Index came in at 50.0 in September from 52.0 in August, which was below the 51.6 consensus figure.

Under the magnificent seven umbrella, shares of Apple (AAPL) were downgraded by Jefferies to Underperform from Hold citing excessive expectations on iPhones and foldable phones.
